review_text: As immersive as you allow it to be. The framework of the game is very outdated, but it gets the job done. Still a lot to be desired (better achievement system would be a great start), but I'm afraid a new game would have to be created to get a much better experience. Addictive, gets you out, gets you walking/running, and there are people playing it almost everywhere if you want to socialize. Amazing game.

Lower remote raid pass prices! It's stupid high now. Go back to 250 coins for 3. It was much better when it was like that. Now it takes a week and a half to save up for three, and buying three doesn't save you coins unless there's a fake discount meant to make you think you're getting a deal even though you aren't because you are still massively over paying. It's ridiculous. The average casual player, which is the vast majority of the player base, agrees because this makes it harder to play.

Have played this game since the day it came out with many highs and lows over the years. But this has become one of the most frustrating periods! I have not been able to play in over a month! The game litterally freezes within 2 seconds of loading. You CAN'T play! There was ONE day in the last 30 that it played no problem and then that night it froze again and has ever since. I've seen so many complaints about it and hope they fix it soon otherwise they will lose countless players forever.

As a long time pokemon fan, the game really is fun and a great way to encourage exercise and exploration. However, it's got a lot of limitations that I wish I could see improvement on-- specifically, scheduling. Many events last only an hour, and all events are exclusively during the day. This may not be an issue for most people, but sadly I work the graveyard shift and sleep during the day, so if I want to participate in raids, community days, or highlight hours, I have to forgo sleep.

Id say the game is still fun and playable. but that's as far as the good things go. The microtransactions are worse than ever, just reviving your Pokémon can be a struggle. its hard just to get together and play it anymore because there's constant issues with someone or everyone not having a raid pass, or having to buy a remote raid pass. its quite annoying and I loved this game when it first came out. I cant give it anything above 3 stars.

When I click on anything in the app the screen goes completely black. I have done a force stop and also uninstalled/reinstalled the app. I even went as far as to restart our router and my phone. There is a limited time on some of the monthly events (that I actually paid for) and I am unable to do anything in the game. I am really frustrated because other family members in the house are not having any issues and one of them has the same phone as me.

Edit: Recently there's been way too many pop-ups. Overall, the game is pretty fun and addicting. The servers are now much more stable than they were when the game first released, which actually makes it playable for more than a few minutes. Still, though, the game has a lot of issues that Niantic needs to address and straighten out. This includes the 3-step bug and balancing out some of the game mechanics (such as gym battles).

Same old story of Pokemon GO... Fun WHEN IT WORKS! Frustrating as hell when it doesn't. . . Thier servers are still trash, unfortunately. Way too many bugs & glitches. Random freezes. Things disappearing or not appearing in the first place. PvP is practically unplayable most of the time. Edit: I have added a star since my original review.

Really fun to play! I love being able to walk around un the real world and see Pokémon wandering about! It dose have some annoying issues though. It's awesome that the game is updated often to keep it running the best it can, but it dose get a bit annoying at times, as it seems to be more often then is probably necessary. There is also a common bug that makes it so you can't click on anything or open any tabs. Other then those few things, its really awesome and is genuinely fun to play

I would give 0 if possible. Don't bother with in app support. The answer to every issue, every bug, every exploit, every issue is to blame your cell connection. Doesn't matter if you're on WiFi/wired or that you didn't have any other symptoms of connection issues. They won't fix or respond to anything, and instead will blame your wireless provider ad nauseum. Checking in a year later: nothing has improved. I do not recommend this game. Scopely does not appear to care about server stability.

Honestly I dislike the game. there are so many issues with the raids. Every time I report, I end up with a pass restored but that doesn't help the fact I missed out on that limited edition Boss or anything because of the error or the distant raid visit. I spend money because I try to keep up with friends, but I really hate the game. Wish it worked better.

Played since release. It was fun for quite a while, but now I just login once in a while, see what around me and log off. Nobody wants to do raids so I dont join them. theres no point in purchasing anything. I think for game longevity things need to change. Maybe solo raids could be a thing. Add items to target farm specific pokemon. That kind of stuff. Hope the game gets better.

The game is okay, but there are always bugs, so it gets frustrating. Sometimes the game will crash just after I catch a pokemon, or I can't open the screen to play with my buddy, or the buddy just can't be placed, or the little photo sticker menu gets stuck on the screen even after I exit. I've had these same problems for years and always end up deleting the app and redownloading a year or two later.

I love playing this game on my breaks, but for the past week it has become so glitchy it's almost impossible to play. constantly getting "network error" or "try again later" at every poke stop. with the recent upgrades. I thought the glitching would stop. I really hope they fix the bugs and make this game fun again.

Started playing the game when it came out. However, this game used to be fun. BUT, by adding TOO MUCH to the game (dynamax, max, gigantimax, etc), it has become too confusing and hard to keep up with. The original "gym format" shouldn't have been changed. If you don't have a pokemon powerful enough to take or defend a gym......find one you can. OR make gyms based on levels or strength of pokemon (tiers). Also, 4 star pokemon and shinies rates should increase after a set level (50+).

All I want for Christmas is a way to sort postcards by alphabetical order so I can easily delete all my doubles. Otherwise its a fun app. I am interested to see how the new updates go. But for the love of all things good in the world, please make it easier for us to delete duplicate post cards. The sorting by sender is broken. If I can sort by alphabetical order, I can sort and see duplicates even if I have doubles from 2 different people. Please do this.

love the app, but I do have a few problems with the app: 1. anytime I'm battling (whether that be against ppl or rocket grunts/bosses) the game will lag so bad that I have to exit the entire app or else it'll freeze the game completely, 2. it takes over 2 minutes for my game to open(yes I timed it.), 3. sometimes when I'm trying to catch Pokémon they'll never catch and instead will just run away. EVERY. SINGLE. ONE, 4. I could catch a 1000 Pokémon with one ball, but then I can't catch a 25??

I used to play this game everyday until the latest update. The app keeps force closing on me. I can be in the middle of a battle and it will suddenly close. It's really frustrating! I'm thinking about uninstalling. Used to be 5 stars for me but now... It sucks Update: I wasn't having any problems up until recently. The game was working just fine but now it's force closing on me nearly every time I open it. Cleared the cache, checked for updates but nothing works. Ugh!

Enjoyed playing this with loved ones. It's quality in terms of mobile app games. When they raised the prices on quality of life items, I wasn't bothered. When Niantic sold the game to a Saudi company, I was only kind of concerned. Even when it was revealed that the game was a data miner, I wasn't that outraged. Recently, the game has added a feature that turns off vpns when you load it up. We're not doing that. I'm leaving a one star review and uninstalling.

a few years ago, I would have given this game more stars,but thats changed ever since Scopely took over. the game itself is good, its just that if you live ina rural area with a smaller player community, expect to have to spend quite a bit of money to actually acquire the better pokemon. if you are in a large city,you are good. they seem to have an issue with rural locations,and while I get it they are out to make money,forcing us rural players to have to buy things, just isn't a way to do it

There's no ads, which is a HUGE win for mobile gaming. I played this when it first released, and I still believe that's when we were the closest to world peace. The downfall of Pokémon GO is the fact that there's SO MUCH going on, but they give you so little to work with. Upgrading storage is slow, expensive, and doesn't give what it's worth. Those extra 50 Pokémon slots? Gone in an hour.

I've been playing since 2019. It used to be better, as many are saying. The current problem with mine is that even though adventure sync is turned on, it's not working. I only collect kms when the game is running (and eating through my battery, even though I'm not playing). My other complaint is that when they redid levels, they gave you tons of items (awesome), then later said, "Oops here's 200 extra storage." Yet, I'm still out of storage from the level change. 🙄

Microtransactions have become more and more expensive. I have no issue buying items: the developers etc need a paycheck just like the rest of us. But prices are higher now than before, just look at the bundles on the store, and ticket prices. Plus, it's borderline pay to win anymore, and lately, there's a paywall if you want a greater chance at adding the latest Pokemon to your Pokedex, not to mention the the bugs & shoddy customer support. There much more to say, but I'm out of room.

I just find there could so easily be more done to streamline the accessibility & make this a worthwhile experience. It feels empty to a degree; pay to win; & the sheer grind can wear on you heavily. Small, user-friendly additional content would go a long way. This game yearns for a Tamagotchi-like component, as well as a way to show off your Pokémon to others. Unfortunately, as it stands, this is as barebones as it gets, & with so many paying so much, it likely will not change. Give a little.

Amazing game! But, with all the raids, battles and events, there's too much going on. I mean.. I love the events, but the different types of raids+battles are too confusing. I lovee shinx. Fav pokemon evolution line. I think there should be a way to lure in specific types of pokemon. Like how there are sandwiches in pokemon scarlet and violet. It would make it easier to get the pokemon you want instead of just hoping to get one.

Update: My pokemon eventually returned from the gym after a couple of weeks however I did see in one of the gyms 2 pokemons that stayed there for over 95 days, this is unacceptable. Another thing is that on android phones you can't redeem codes in the app and have to go in a website unlike iPhone users who can redeem it in app in the poke store. This is a decent game however I'd like these problems to be evaluated. Please fix this Niantic.

Buggy as hell, when an update comes in you can't play until you install it. After the last update I can no longer log in. The game was awesome at first but has become more and more frustrating to the point where it's not worth it anymore. update: it got worse. They've turned the game into a micro-transaction nightmare, a total pay-to-play. There's 3 level 50 trainers who will not allow anyone to takeover a gym. I can't feed berries to my Pokemon when a gym is being raided, but they can and do

Came back after years away and some things have changed for better, but the constant lockouts to update and reinstall is excessive [7/16/16] A LOT of fun ...when it works. Highly addictive, but I'll pass until the glitches get worked out with the server. It freezes WAY too much!! And, of course, always at the worst times. Also want to see battles with strangers (not in the gym), pokemon trading and have the pedomer running when the app is closed.

All of the reviews citing leveling update woes are 100% accurate and correct. Rollouts are done terribly! I've never seen an app take up so much space and have so many updates but nothing actually improves. The goals want you to catch 20, 30, 40 pokémon in a day or a week or a month, but you don't get half the items to do that with, so it's geared heavily toward pay to play now. I have to go through periods of inactivity because of a limited storage space for Pokemon. Shame. It could be good.

Great game, gives good incentives to go outside and enjoy nature. Nothing can ever take away the rush when you find a shiny or a really rare pokemon in the wild. One of the only problems with the game is that it is entirely location based. You either have a good place to play and can succeed, or you have a bad place and can't do much in game without traveling a few miles away. Another problem is that the game is moving more and more to micro transactions, with multiple passes and paid content.

I am quite addicted to this game, unfortunately. I get a lot of enjoyment out of it. My problem is the company in general. I bought remote raid passes with real money. I completed a raid but the app froze before I could get to the catch encounter. I filed a complaint, and a bot responded that there was no problem and they would not reimburse me. I have since found out this happens regularly. It would cost them nothing to fix this, but they choose to do the wrong thing instead.

The game constantly freezes and has to be closed and reopened. When doing raids, the game will freeze or kick you out when going into the encounter to catch the raid boss. This has been constantly happening for months. I have tried multiple suggested resolutions from Niantic with no change in functionality across multiple devices. UPDATE: Now freezing even more frequently and the latest update doesn't show icons right. DO YOU EVEN TEST ANYTHING? Every time there is a change something breaks.

Cool game, I've been looking forward to playing but glitches many times. Servers down most of the day. Hope it gets fixed because it won't be worth keeping if servers are down more than half the day. updated review. game is still very bad, they require you to spend all kinds of money on remote passes and then the game freezes not allowing you to catch the Pokémon you spent money trying to catch just for support to tell you they will not reimburse your passes what a scam....

Boned by the update, sure I am a higher level and I received literally 1000's of items and a few upgrades. However, I cannot get new presents, I cannot claim rewards for the battle league, I cannot do a lot of things until I use over 1000 items or delete them... All that and I am still unable to get regional Pokemon from friend gifts?! Sure,I can maybe get a scatterbug, but seriously, while I love the update the item dump was ridiculous without also upgrading storage by a lot more than they did.

Has been a motivator in going for walks. Edit: I had to dock stars after playing it longer. While I still use it as a fun diversion while walking, the limitations on the inventories for Pokemon, items, & postcards are incredibly frustrating. Coupled with your ability to earn the cash shop currency being capped at 50/day, it takes far too long to fix. Long distance friends can't trade & have to buy special raid passes for $$ in order to battle together. Too many microtransactions/pay-to-win.

This app is so neat and it keeps me active, but the glitches/bugs are unreal. Over the last couple days, it has been cheating me out of km walked with my buddy. GPS errors for no obvious reason, gym errors, sound cutting out randomly, etc. - all problems I've had within the last two weeks, let alone over the 9+ years I've been playing. A game with such expensive in-app purchases should offer a higher quality experience.

Great game, when it works. Numerous issues with the Pokemon Go Plus peripheral, unable to authenticate issues (I have an account that have not been able to log into during Dec 2019),failure to log in, lost connections, Google Fit not transferring data, etc. Couple that with barely responsive customer support, would be best to skip this one. Almost 6 years later, some of these issues have been resolved and some additional things have been added (remote raid, better friends' interactions, routes).

I have been playing this game since it originally launched and I really enjoy some of the changes that have been made over the years. One biggest complaint now is that the play with your pokemon feature has always been difficult to work with, and that's hasn't improved. It tells you to look for an open flat surface but then never gives me to foot steps to place a pokemon making leveling friendship and gathering gifts very difficult.

The game is a lot of fun when the app works. It's pretty glitchy. I sometimes have to open and close it repeatedly to get it to load my location. The layout of the app needs attention. I can never find my rewards because they're scattered across four or five menus plus a couple of other places I wouldn't think to click. Having everything in one place would actually make sense.

Loving the game besides the problem after defeating gyms. When you win, the loading screen afterwards is way too long and other people take the gym from you, even when it says it doesn't happen. I've lost multiple gyms because of this and didn't have the power to defeat it all over again. If you get out of the loading screen quicker, same thing happens. Very disappointing, please change it.

After nearly a decade and many reworks later, this game is significantly easier to get back into and feel free to do as you want. There's tons of events going on frequently to give options for availability when going out to participate catching stuff you want. Use this as an excuse to get outside and go on a long walk chasing pokemon. It's fun.

Now the recent update/upgrade is so good that you have to click on a gift multiple times before it will let you open it. guess it wants to make sure you want your friends gift? the new layout also is sus and was not broken so they decided it needed to be fixed for their own amusement. add the lag, glitching and inability to smoothly transition from WiFi to cell data, this is the game for you! Don't do anything you want to keep going from WiFi to cell data. it will crash just about every tim.

Still a really fun (and addicting) game with more to do than ever. We all know what this game is at this point, so I'm not going to go into all the stuff you can do. However, I'm taking a star off for two reasons. One, after all this time, they still never solved the issue of the game never reconnecting if you lose your connection in the middle of catching a pokémon. And two, some features aren't well explained and involve far too much messing around to try to figure them out.
super raids are terrible. that said, at least they seemed to comp a reasonable amount of mega energy.

I love playing Pokémon Go! I have been playing since 2019 and have been off and on over the years. It pains me to drop a 3 star because I recently got into playing more battles, and let me tell you, I have never been more disappointed in a online matchmaking system in my entire life! Loss after Loss and it seems like every time I hop in a battle my opponent has too many counters. Now I know that fixing a matchmaking system is a lot of work, but for the love of God make it more rewarding to play.

Played on & off again since release in 2016. Game use to be amazing, everyone you saw played the game, it was fun to play for all ages. I got back on after quite a few months, maybe a year & there were significant changes that just seemed to change the aspect of the game🫤 You would think after nearly a decade of playing, you'd be close to catching them all but they keep creating new Pokémon that didn't even exist in the mainline. As i get older, this just isn't for me anymore. It has been fun

It's come a loooooong way since it's glitchy & limited 1st gen days, but there's still a lot more work to be done. PVP needs a balancing overhaul, the events end too quickly with rewards that don't warrant the rushed effort, and prices have almost doubled on passes of all sorts. At this point, I only continue playing because of all the time & money I've dumped into this game over the years, and it's an extra way for me and my girlfriend to bond.

Absolutely ridiculous to discontinue support for 32 bit phones. I'm not doing anything else on my phone that demands more processing power than I have, and I'm certainly not buying a new and more expensive phone just to play a game. Already excessively resource intensive, even when it was first released, but I always excused it because it would at least run on my devices. Terrible way to handle mobile game development

I tried to open the app. It prompts me to clear the cache, so I select that. It "optimizes" the app and then an "open" option is available. Once I click that it lets me know that Pokemon Go has a bug and once the developers fix the bug, I can update the app and try again. I paid for the Bug Out event... are we gonna get back in before this event ends?!? Not to mention I have daily tasks that I can't do. Do such and such this many days in a row. If I have to start those again I'm gonna lose it!

I really enjoy Pokemon Go and have been playing since the release in 2016. Its a good excuse to get out and spend some time outside to "touch grass". However, the Android app has significantly more issues than the iOS app does. I have to sign out and back in on average about 4 times per week and deal with freezes daily. This would be a 5 star review if the app was more flushed out, but given the issues I have seemingly just playing the game I can't give more than 3.

This game has gone downhill hard. The best years are behind it, which sucks as this game was actually good. It started going downhill after the pandemic. The changes to the remote raid pass, the newer pay to win events, & of course the selling of the game to Scopely. All that among other things make this game no longer worth playing in 2025... which is extremely sad. I highly suggest all players save their Pokemon by transferring them to Pokemon Home then uninstall this game & never look back.

I love this game with a burning passion. My only issue with it really is when there's one pokemon in the middle of a couple others that you CANNOT check unless you capture the ones around it. There has to be some sort of fix for this. It's not a major problem just annoying when you want to see the pokemon and have to go through catching the others just to check it.

Just a bad game. Brings out the worst in people. Creators claim it supports community, but all it does is divide it. And like 8 out of 10 times you go to a gym it gives "gym under construction" error which has been going for months! They keep adding more to it but cannot even fix the most basic functions of the game, like throwing a pokeball...Just horribly managed and then they lie and capitalize on errors they create. Could be such a great game but it's not. Too bad.

This current version removes the navigation bar whole sale, this means I cannot use the home button or swap apps, or even use the back button on certain screens. Please consider instead an option to choose between. Doing so without asking trainers feels like a bad move in my opinion. The flee button is on the upper left hand corner which is far more inconvenient for catching in a full-screen mode.

YIKES I played this game on release on my old phone, OnePlus 3. It heated up and drained the battery like crazy 8 years later I play it on my new phone, OnePlus 9. It heats up and drains the battery like crazy Sometimes the prompt buttons become unresponsive, it doesn't see the GPS, and continues having persistent issues I would imagine a game continuously developed for 8 years would address. Especially on the optimization side, since I don't even use the AR functionality

Was asked how likely I was to recommend on a scale, but didn't ask "why" when I put 0. I am very and getting more so regularly with this nonsense where I use the touch screen to toss the ball strait and 80% of the time, no matter what I do, it curves, veers off to the side ... And unlike regular pokemon games no way to easily have the ball thrown strait... It's not "fun" when it's a struggle to get the ball to go straight, much less catch a pokemon... It scores a 10/10 for the irritation factor

The new update that took away the navigation bar has made it so much harder to play the game. The developer seems to have forgotten that newer phones don't have back buttons and you have to swipe from the edge to go back. With the new update, you can't swipe to go back, despite this being possible in previous versions. It ends up wasting Pokeballs if you try to use your phone's normal method to go back.

The game is great overall, but I'm entirely unable to use AR mode. I tried looking up why this might be, but to no luck. My old phone was able to run AR mode, and now it can't. I have a new phone and it still can't run it. I apparently need an app called ARCore to run Pokémon Go AR. I have a built-in gyroscope and everything, so it's definitely the game. My camera keeps turning on and off and my screen is black. Everything on my phone is up to date, too

fun to play when it actually works but at this point it's time to uninstall because the game is so slow, glitch and laggy. on a good day I'll reboot the game and it will work as intended for a few minutes but on most days you'll sit in loading screen for almost 10 mins before quitting (minding that I've got great internet & my larger games that require more power still work seamlessly in between the issues I have with pokego) or have the game freeze in the middle of your adventure. a real shame

Been playing for years. Got a new phone, worked for a week, and now will never come up again. I click on the play option it loads then gets to the screen where I press on or to where announcements are, and then it shuts down again. If they fix it, I'll change it.

Certain features like routes are still INCREDIBLY buggy even after a long time. Which wouldn't be a problem is evolutions weren't locked behind it. I can not recommend this app at this stage because it doesn't feel like the devs adds even trying to fix the issue. And THAT is why I have it such a low score.

Editing this to a one-star for one simple reason: They bricked the game on 32 bit android, and not everyone has the cash for a new phone with a better OS. Probably done with this franchise for good, and Skylanders was a better game series as a kid anyway.

I've been playing this game since 2019 (6 years) and it feels like every step forward they take 2 steps back. They focus all of their efforts on monetizing the game instead of making it fun, they do cool things, but they work against the games balance. At it's core it was a game about exploring, and having fun, but they turned it into a game about starting at your phone in big cities rather than exploring. Stopped playing for a year when their was a legal discriminination issue in April, 2024

Thos is ok but seem to get a lot of bugs & glitches. The rewards & shadow pokemon seem to be the same most of the time & Pokemon in the wild seem to be less in my area than in other areas. the remote pass seems to be very rare as I don't seem to get one.This is why I rate it a 3 star mostly on the glitches. As I have to keep downloading the app

Decent gameplay, BUT be careful not to fall into traps - Niantic quite often has to deal with shortcomings in their programming. When these treat specific offers like tickets for all kinds of events and users cannot use Pokémon GO in its full functionality, Niantic fails to take responsibility. Reimbursements of purchases are excluded categorically, even though they admittedly cannot provide the promoted services. This should be taken into account, especially when considering in-app purchases.

A fast game is a good game, speed up animations please. Starting to get too busy with too many things going on, one cant do it all. Also getting buggy with heart points not appearing or remote trade notifications popping up every 15 seconds. Bulk trading, 10-20 mon at a time calculating the greatest distance and candy automatically would be good to save some time.

I've been playing this game since release and it feels like for every step forward, they take two steps back. They've done some cool things but seem to be actively working against their own game. At its core, the game was about getting out and exploring. They've turned it into a game about congregating in big cities to block sidewalk traffic and stare at phones. They've focused all effort on monetizing and none on the fun. There's no incentive to explore anymore. I no longer recommend this game.

Alright, but I really wish there was some type of option for offline. Also, I've noticed this very frustrating issue that has only started happening recently. When I go to delete my (gifts) instead of, you know, pressing the little trash can 🗑, then okay, I end up having to repeat that last step multiple times the most times I've had to press okay just to delete ONE (gift) was 10 times!!!!! plz fix this idk what is the problem but plz fix it 🙏🏻 😩 😫

Has a bad/ recurring issue with the game that is letting the player know that "a remote trade has expired" even though the trade was completed. Issues started 3-6-26. Gives a in-game notification every 30-40ish seconds. Makes the game hard to play with constant notification. Please fix. Awesome besides recurring issues.

Play daily still but think I'm done Paying because since it switched or w.e went on, I can't even collect my reward road items, can't do anything with Ninitac, & I had $400 in pending charges while you guys were doing the changes b.c they weren't working but you still held it on the card. Please fix the issues so I can have my game back. I live on a super small area so it's also very hard to do much! I'd love easier options for spin spots, raid spots, ect.

I mean, it could be better. pokemon shouldn't stay at a gym for more than 15 days, I've seen pokémon in remote gyms sitting there for months! that's really inconvenient. Also, battling shouldn't just be a fast way to give yourself carpal tunnel. It's super boring, all you do is tap a dot until it fills, flail your finger across the screen to pop more dots, and then you go back to tapping the charge button till your finger hurts. boring and physically painful, wonderful combo niantic.

The last problem was fixed. Now I didnt get to a remote trade in time and it expired, so now about every 5 minutes it reminds me that I missed it with a big box that comes up and blocks me from what I am doing. Come on please fix that I love this game and want to play not tap a button that says ok. I dont even know how to list it if I report a bug problem.

There needs to be a communication function on this app, it's long overdue, and it should have been here in the very beginning. If you want to make sure you have enough people in a raid or want to trade specific Pokémon, you have to use outside factors in order to communicate amongst other players remotely. Speaking of, there should be some way to not cost you a remote raid pass because others jump out of raids that you can't beat without a certain amount of players in it.

I love playing this game on my breaks, but for the past week it has become so glitchy it's almost impossible to play. constantly getting "network error" or "try again later" at every poke stop. with the recent upgrades. I thought the glitching would stop. I really hope they fix the bugs and make this game fun again.

since the lastest update, it haw been freezing and lagging constantly. I cant open gifts, when I use items in bag to heal pokemon it doesn't do anything. you can never complete a 'challenge' it resets the kms walked when your buddy needs to be fed to be back on the screen, and it seems to keep happening @ 0.2km left in challeng. It is constantly telling me to try again when getting pokestops, then it doesn't work.

Good game overall, but there are some very serious issues that should've been fixed by now the main 3 being that ur pokemon can be a at a gym for an absurd amount of time with no way if getting them back, Raids being hard to access especially remotely. And the game being very diffrent for people that live in a rural area compared to a city.

THIS IS RIDICULOUS! After this "little update" I can no longer sign into my account any longer, I have to download a plethora of applications for signing up or recovery and receiving codes to input and yet I still don't have access to my account. I've only started playing a week and this one update ruined the whole experience.

A well designed Pokémon game. It is an easy game to play with friends,yet it is also good at respecting your offline privacy. New things are constantly being added to the game, making it so it's almost never boring.My only complaint is that you can't gift the deluxe versions of the the Go Pass to friends. Also, the game freezes up and gliches more than I would prefer, but I have learned how to cope. Pokémon Go is a game that I hope will be continued and revised for a long time to come.

This game is extremely predatory. It is clearly designed in a way that forces players to have to spend money. It is pretty obvious catch rates are severely stunted, with some low level pokemon taking 20+ balls to catch. It is an absolute joke what this game has become. It's a shame because it used to be very enjoyable..Now it feels rigged..

This game is still one of the best Pokemon games to this day, I keep coming back to it and have been since the first day it released. The game is fun and addictive, there is so much you can do such as raids, catch pokemon, battle, trade, shiny hunt, explore real places in real life and In game and even create poke stops for communities to enjoy!

I love this app but it is starting to become annoying how I am not getting the weekly achievement of how many km I have walked during 1 week and it also not calculating the km for the buddy and eggs either. I even restarted the whole sync when I seen this happening the first time and its still not happening.

not only is this only designed for people in big cities but it's constantly lagging no matter how good the internet is and it's constantly telling me the raid has expired even though I can see the time left and I had just gotten the invite. and this is the second time you've duped me into buying a go fest ticket to get a mythical that you them give away for free(but not for the people who actually paid you, we only get 25 candy instead of a second) there won't be a third time, done giving money.

Love pokemon go but the lag when using incense. The lack of pokeballs when opening friends gifts with the amount of useless items instead is rather frustrating. timed events still suck. still using alot of wasted items to catch pokemon that ain't worth keeping. the website store doesn't let me collect the items for the journey road. in all things still haven't improved

This app hasn't worked on Pixel phones for months. E-mailing Niantic just gets an automated reply saying to contact them in the app, which crashes as soon as you tap on anything. January 2026 update still hasn't fixed the issue. March 5: app is even worse now and crashes immediately after opening.

Recently, got back into this game with my kids. Its very fun to play together and get them active. I think my only complaint is completing the passes and being "rewarded" with Pokémon encounters that constantly escape the pokeballs is hardly rewarding. Its aggravating wasting pokeballs and something that is supposed to be a reward.

Games becoming unplayable if you're a rural player. My village is small and we've lost about 5 stops/gyms in last few months. With it the ability to do challenges, raids and routes. No explanation on removals or appeals. Honestly terrible been playing 10 years and really losing interest in this game

"gotta catch the null" Overheats so bad I can't even hold my phone and it shuts it down. When I try to catch a pokémon, the screen turns completely black and doesn't load. Gets worse with every new update. I've played the game for 10 years off and on. I hope this gets fixed

it was ok. the game was quit glitchy, sometimes when I go to catch a pokemon. I throw the poke ball, and it escapes right then, then I runs away. the next day it said I caught the pokemon. even though it escaped last time. I hope it this will look ahead your purchase. also updates keep coming over and over. so annoying! 😠

Fix the numerous issues with Pokémon nicknames. I can't even reset Poke names to default anymore and the recent update has ruined every single one of the nicknames I use to quickly identify core information like movesets and IVs. Just let us use unicode characters why even remove this that has been in the game since the start, how am I even supposed to quickly identify this core information if the game hasn't bothered at all to implement any of this themselves?

Edit: This is still an issue! The game constantly freezes or gives a "GPS not found" error, every day, multiple times a day. The app needs to be restarted multiple times every day. This is not a device issue as GPS works fine for other apps, but many users keep getting plagued with this error constantly. Now there's an issue where certain special characters no longer show up, ruining many people's organization/remaining on thousands of pokemon

So the most recent update is pushing some new BS ARtech that now prevents me from being able to interact with a buddy Pokémon. Previous versions of the app didn't require this jump and I can comfortably say since that game has not suddenly made some major graphical or QOL jump with this update, there's no real reason to do this other than screwing over your player base. Which unfortunately is quite the norm for mobile game devs. So if that's not the case, prove me and wrong fix your Game.

This game has been completely ruined by how much it has moved to pay to play over the last three years and that's really disappointing because I was originally really looking forward to playing this with my children as they grew. If I could give the game a 0 star review I would. After the Kalos event, I will no longer be playing with any regularity and only because the Kalos event ticket was purchased for me and I do not want to waste more money. It makes me sad because I played almost everday

Great game but it hurts my eyes when I play, even if its during the day. I always have to turn my phone brightness way low. Please add a dark mode, or at least a way to disable all the flashing! I'm not epileptic, but the UI is a very bright white and every little interaction triggers that dumb flash effect. It feels like im getting raptured every second I play. It's also really easy to hit the pokemon/item storage limits which can chase off newcomers at worst and is just annoying. Please fix!

I'm resorting to writing a review because unfortunately this app has the worst customer service interface I've ever seen. If You can't get into the app, there is no way to contact support. Tried the email, got an automated message saying to use the app. There is no way to speak to an actual human being. Your app is broken and I can't even tell you because I can't get into the app. FIX YOUR CUSTOMER SUPPORT, HIRE CUSTOMER SUPPORT EMPLOYEES!!!

This app is a good game, but only barely. It's not because Niantic are at all competent as designers or developers; the game's core concept is just so good that they couldn't ruin it if they tried (though they do try, and certainly come close). It's a great way to get oneself out and about -- healthy -- but the experience is a blend of chill, low-key fun and boiling, caustic frustration. The monetization is fine, but the money players are spending clearly isn't going into improving the game.

20+ Friends Required for Key Features For years, I enjoyed playing the game with my partner. We would go to the park or even the city for special events and get a lot accomplished However, with Dynamax and now the new super Mega pokemon, the game requires you to play with 20+ people, not an exaggeration meaning despite seeing cool pokemon everywhere, you cannot play the game. It's special event only. Pokemon Go was nice because you could play it when you want and be rewarded. Not anymore

It is insulting that players are expected to grind toward a level 80 cap while Niantic barely invests in basic game stability or releasing the hundreds of missing Pokémon from years ago. Between the ancient bugs and the lack of content, the game has devolved into a blatant pay-to-win scheme where progress is locked behind endless paid research and expensive event tickets. This feels less like a game and more like a massive, unpolished chore designed solely to drain your wallet and time.

I've played the game since it came out and I think it is an absolute blast. I LOVE raiding with friends, catching pokemon and hunting shiny pokemon. One problem i have is with raid passes being so freaking expensive lmao like I have to grind to get ONE. Also you kinda need friends to get like every legendary but other than that the game is a blast!!! highly recommend to people who are trying to get into pokemon :3 -DarknerOfScales

I absolutely love this app but it needs a major update as trying to catch pokemon or battle team rocket is really difficult as it keeps going off with network error but my network is just fine and the area is also. I have re-downnloaded the app, restarted it etc makes no difference. I should have evolved my charcadet ages ago but its impossible at the moment not being able to battle

your game sucks. i used to ply everyday and spend money on the game and loved it. you are so focused with pushing new material and raising prices and making the game a pay to play game that no attention has been put to fixing bugs and problems with the game. constantly reseting and glitching. getting kicked out of raids or not being allowed to catch pokemon u beat in raids after u have to spent real money to get the passes in the first place. spend more time fixing issues, less time taking money

Love the new pokemon they added in the super mega raids. however i really hate how theyve been executing the recent updates for the past few months. every update is filled with more things to purchase instead of making the game more fun and enjoyable. The game feels so empty now despite all they keep adding, Its hard to do anything in this game when the only way to reliably get things done is to either spend money or download other apps to help you.

Disappointment of a game, I try to friend someone, and actually do friend someone, yet it doesn't show them on the friend menu? That's the first thing, second thing is menu issues, either it lags like no tomorrow or it just kicks me out of the game ( and it's definitely not my Wifi doing it ). The third thing, is whenever I try catching a pokemon, 90% of the time the ball will go anywhere but where I threw it, I try throwing straight, it goes completely left, I try throwing right, nope... Sad

Lost 2 remote raid passes without even joining the battles. I clicked on the RSVP and it took a pass. Can't even check to see who is in the battle without losing a pass. If you don't dump money into this game, you get nothing worth your time. They have made it to where pay to play is the only way to play properly and get good stuff. If you free play, good luck.

they're still just as greedy, if not MORE under Scopely, Super Mega Raids that combine the WORST features of Max Battles and Rocket Raids that will likely lock out any new Mega Evolutions they add to the game will cause people to drop the game in no time, I took an extended break over the past 9-10 months and it's becoming more apparent that if you're not spending money on events, then you're not the people that they want to play the game, just buy a Switch and the main series games.

What the hell happened? I've been a Pokemon GO fan since its release and recently came back after not playing for a few years. This game is Pay-To-Win, overly complicated and *insanely* buggy. This used to be "go for a walk, catch some Pokemon, grab stuff from PokeStops and call it a day", now you have too many functions and "features" and most of them aren't even working. This is just sad.

as far as the app is concerned I'm happy the gameplay for me is very smooth and fun. but the reward road feature sucks the store isn't easy to navigate, it makes it difficult to find anything and redirects you to the online store instead of making it in the app it's horrible.

I used to like the game. now I can't collect anything on reward road. when I was able to since I started playing. actually enjoyed this game better the 90's Gameboy version. but im realizing i enjoyed the Gameboy one better. at least if it glitches all you had to do was the cartridge out blow on it then restart from last save

this game rips you off, it costs money for remote raid passes but the catch rate is so low on some of the Pokémon that you waste passes which is a waste of money I tried for the same one twice and with platinum medals, golden raspberries, great or better curve balls I still didn't catch it 40 balls and nothing this wasted 2 of my remote passes (unacceptable) the catch rate should be guaranteed or at least set way higher than 2% absolute waste of money.

Pokémon Go is certainly a successful project, both for free time and for the eternal routine. Whichever side you look at, the game turned out to be a completely reasonable and strategically convenient option, both for those who love walking and similarly for those who remember and love the universe of the same name. Thanks to this game, I met several interesting people, caught the excitement of collecting "shiny" and of course took part in more than one massive raid.

If you really want to waste your time, download this game. Grinding without spending money feels impossible. It claims to be free to play, but progress is painfully slow unless you pay. Raids require many local players, which is extremely hard to find in my area in India. PvP could be fun, but one turn of lag ruins everything. The system feels broken and unstable. I wanted to love this game, but between paywalls, lack of players, and constant lag, it becomes more frustrating than enjoyable 😢

Can't claim my Reward Road gifts I earned through purchases. web store doesn't even load for me it just sends me to the News portion of the website no matter what shop option I selected. tried to contact support but there's no option to contact them about Reward Roads issues so I tried to submit other tickets under other issues. I just get a bunch of premade options to choose from and can't tell the chat what my actual problem is. Reward Road is broken.

Came to drop a bad review. you removed 4 valid pokestops in my area that OTHER people submitted, after I submitted something and it was rejected. I don't know why your team of investigators continue to make the game HARDER for people. The game is meant to get you outside and walking. Not in the car. You should leave the stops as is and add MORE criteria for new stops. Highly gatekept game.

buggier than usual. this is the most severely buggy game I've ever played and latest update it's somehow worse, it freezes during every battle, forcing me to lose every battle, making it pointless to play in the league. none of my other games crash more than yearly and this game crashes literally 10-15x per day every day also you gotta stop forcing the popup telling players about the remote tag on me constantly. I have had it over 300x!! every single player knows about it, stop nagging me!!!

Edit: the app shuts down my music app while playing so I have nothing to listen to and they don't have good in game music and it's repetitive. I have been DENIED raid bosses multiple times. I waste over half my golden raspberries on every throw, I throw great and excellent and it takes all the balls and still nothing. I am routinely cheaped out of my charged moves in all battles. it's to the point it looks like my account is being deliberately hindered. it's too much to be coincidence.

I like the game its pretty fun, at the end of the day it makes exercising enjoyable. the game can be easily tempting to spend money. but unless you really like the game there's no reason to spend money on it the normal pokemon are free to catch. also catching pokemon in raids is a skill thing. just hit every throw with a great or excellent throw other wise it will definitely run and use a golden raz. save your money dont remote raid, essentially a scam to take advantage of lazy people

i would give at least 3 stars minimum however its kind of hard to do that when support sucks when you are forced to use the app itself for help, but it keep restarting when you have to upload picture evidence for help have them close your cause because you couldn't upload the picture because of the app, which seems to be a wide ranging issue for others. Then when you use the email they posted here to get help, you get an auto response to use the method that is trash and wont work.

This used to be a great game and really helped people to get out and be social. over the years, this game got better as new things were introduced. then free events became paid events. then they got expensive. now there is a LOT of stuff that's pay walled and everything is a micro transaction. the game also has become less playable. often it hangs or fails to load. it's a shame to see a once incredible free game be reduced to a pay to a joyless pay to win turd thanks to greedy developers

Fun but buggy. You'll be logged out if you leave the app open and use a VPN, even if it's just in the background. You can use wi-fi, just never with a VPN. When you're logged out, you have to use one of three browsers (inbuilt, chrome, or safari) to log back in: anything else is kicked back including firefox. Aside from these technical issues, it's fun: all pokemon are available to catch. Worth the issues.

I like the game but I can't open it or find an easy bug report button so it's gotta be a 1-star for now. the game loads up the Niantic screen then stops short of opening anything to do with Pokemon, instead stopping at a black screen at the time of writing. hope that helps fix the issue.

I used to love this game so much, my brother and I are always playing every event but lately the game keeps crashing in the middle of a match, literally crashed 3 times, in one raid today, doesn't seem like anything is getting done to fix it, honestly debating if to even keep it installed
